basics.name: Sadjad Alikhani
basics.label: Ph.D. Student in Electrical Engineering
basics.summary: Ph.D. student at Arizona State University working with Professor Ahmed Alkhateeb on wireless foundation models, digital twins, and data-centric evaluation for communication and sensing.

work.name: Arizona State University, Tempe, United States
highlights: Focus: Foundation models for wireless channels, digital twin aided systems, and dataset similarity.

work.name: Nokia Bell Labs, Murray Hill, United States (Remote)
highlights: Developed a large-scale multimodal digital twin dataset pipeline.
Used the dataset to pretrain environment-aware foundation models for communication and sensing.

work.name: University of Erlangen-Nuremberg, Erlangen, Germany (Remote)
highlights: Designed and simulated algorithms for RIS-aided MIMO communication under limited CSI.

volunteer.organization: Arizona State University, Tempe, United States
highlights: Co-organized the ITU Large Wireless Models challenge.
Prepared the competition package, supported participants, and evaluated submissions from 65 teams.

volunteer.organization: Arizona State University, Tempe, United States
highlights: Journals: IEEE TCCN, IEEE TCOM, IEEE TSP, IEEE TWC.

volunteer.summary: Courses: Neural Networks, Advanced Communication Theory
highlights: Prepared a hands-on introduction to reinforcement learning with Python.
Designed assignments and graded work for more than 80 students.

volunteer.organization: Ferdowsi University of Mashhad, Mashhad, Iran
volunteer.summary: Courses in circuits, signals and systems, communication systems, electrical engineering, and general mathematics
highlights: Supported weekly sessions, graded assignments, and prepared quizzes and projects.

education.institution: Arizona State University, Tempe, United States

courses: Thesis: Joint active and passive beamforming via reduced channel state information in reconfigurable intelligent surface assisted multiuser multiple input multiple output systems.

education.institution: Ferdowsi University of Mashhad, Mashhad, Iran
courses: Thesis: Detection, demodulation, and displaying digital video broadcasting terrestrial television signals with software defined radio.

awards.title: Emerging Talents Initiative Grant
awards.summary: Awarded for academic excellence and research potential.

awards.title: Finalist, Iranian Electrical Engineering Olympiad
awards.summary: Recognized as a finalist in the national competition.

publications.summary: S. Alikhani and A. Alkhateeb, pp. 1-6.

publications.summary: S. Alikhani, G. Charan, and A. Alkhateeb.

publications.publisher: Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers

publications.summary: S. Alikhani and A. Alkhateeb, pp. 796-800.

projects.name: Precoding with reduced CSI in RIS-aided MIMO
projects.summary: Studied precoding strategies for RIS-assisted MIMO systems under limited CSI using convex optimization tools.

projects.name: Proactive eavesdropping in RIS-assisted communications
projects.summary: Developed optimization-based methods for proactive eavesdropping using alternating optimization and semidefinite relaxation.

projects.name: Music genre classification and clustering
projects.summary: Built a dataset of Persian songs, extracted features, and performed supervised and unsupervised learning for music genre analysis.

projects.name: Low-complexity non-convex optimization
projects.summary: Designed an RL-based algorithm for non-convex optimization in RIS-aided communications and compared it with conventional methods.

projects.name: Simulation of signal processing papers
projects.summary: Reproduced published results on semi-blind channel estimation and adaptive beamforming with MATLAB-based simulations.

projects.name: Smart restaurant model
projects.summary: Designed a prototype with a website, RFID-based entry, database-backed ordering, and a customer notification pipeline.

projects.name: Detection and demodulation of DVB-T signals
projects.summary: Implemented demodulation, error correction, and decompression of digital video broadcasting terrestrial signals using software defined radio.

projects.name: Network design
projects.summary: Designed and simulated network architectures with VLANs, spanning tree, subnetting, trunking, FTP, and OSPF routing in Cisco Packet Tracer.
